source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

market_ready_claim_support: Specialty boundary, business outcome, owned workflows, owned artifacts, adjacent lanes, and refusal triggers are explicit in manifest.yaml.
Authority sources include public NIST, CISA, CIS, OWASP, SEC, and FTC sources with authority rationale, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date.
Research summary records workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario family coverage, and non-blocking ambiguity.
Functionality map declares all shared abilities, intake-access-and-initiation abilities, and cross-industry profile ability with passing scenario coverage.
Evaluation results exceed the 12-scenario and 0.92 pass-rate thresholds with no unreviewed regressions.
Deployment and commercialization notes state specification-based runtime and promotion criteria before retaining a retained implementation.

residual_risks.risk: Tenant-specific legal or regulatory precedence can change answers.
residual_risks.mitigation: The manifest and scenarios require legal/compliance routing for mandatory statutory interpretation or filing decisions.

residual_risks.risk: Cross-industry scope can drift into generic best practice.
residual_risks.mitigation: The package requires concrete tenant records, systems of record, and industry-specific caveats before substantive work.

residual_risks.risk: Policy exception support can be mistaken for approval.
residual_risks.mitigation: The boundary requires routing residual-risk and exception approval to accountable owners.

notes: validated status is supported for a specification-based package; no trusted built implementation is claimed.
Human verification records package completeness and consistency, not runtime production execution.
The package should be refreshed when any authority source reaches its decay interval or materially changes.
